title: Reading & Reactions to Online Fiction
summary_en: A study about how people read and react to short online fiction. You'll read one short story and share your impressions. About 13–15 minutes.
summary_zh: 一项关于人们如何阅读并回应网络短篇小说的研究。您将阅读一篇短篇小说并分享您的感受。约 13–15 分钟。

pages.title: Information & consent
pages.html:
You are invited to take part in a research study about how people read and react to short works of online fiction. It is run by a university research team.

What you'll do

  • Read one short story (about 1,200 words) on a page styled like an online fiction archive.
  • Answer questions about your reactions to the story and its author.
  • The whole task takes about 13–15 minutes.

Your data

  • Participation is voluntary; you may stop at any time without penalty.
  • We record your answers and basic interaction timing (e.g., how long pages are open). We do not collect your name. Your participant ID from the recruitment platform is replaced by an irreversible code on our server.
  • Please do not enter any personal information in free-text boxes.

Approved under university research-ethics procedures. Your data is handled in line with the data-protection law that applies where you live (for example the UK/EU GDPR, China's PIPL, or Malaysia's PDPA). Contact details are provided on the debrief page at the end.
pages.checkbox: I am 18 or older, I have read the above, and I consent to take part.

pages.title: A couple of quick questions
pages.screenout_html: Thank you for your interest. This particular study is looking for people who read fiction at least monthly, so it isn't a match this time. You are free to return your submission on Prolific without penalty.

items.text: How often do you read fiction (novels, short stories, fan fiction, etc.)?

pages.title: Your reactions to the story
pages.subtitle: There are no right or wrong answers — we want your honest impression.

blocks.title: If this were on a fiction archive…

items.text: If I saw this story on a fiction archive, I would leave it kudos (a ‘like’).
I would leave a comment on this story.
I would bookmark this story to find it again.

blocks.title: How you found the experience of reading it

items.text: It was fun for me to read this story.
I had a good time reading this story.
The story was entertaining.
The story is well written.
The writing is vivid and engaging.
The story is polished and carefully crafted.
Suppose the archive distributes a $1.00 tip pool among its authors this week. How much of it should this author receive for this story?

blocks.title: This author seems…
blocks.instructions: Based only on the story you just read, how well do these describe the author?

items.text: This author seems warm.
This author seems sincere.
This author seems good-natured.
This is a check that you are reading carefully — for this item, please select ‘Somewhat agree’.
This author seems competent.
This author seems capable.
This author seems skillful.
How much effort did the author put into this story?
How much time do you think the author spent creating this story?
The author invested a great deal of work in this story.
This story feels like a genuine expression of the author’s own voice.
The author seems to have written this out of genuine passion for the story.
This story is truly the author’s own creation.
The story feels manufactured rather than genuinely created.

pages.title: Finally, a little about you
pages.subtitle: These help us describe who took part. Answer as you wish.

items.text: I identify with the fan-fiction / online-fiction community.
Do you have an account on AO3 or a similar fiction archive?
I am interested in using artificially intelligent systems in my daily life.
Artificial intelligence can help solve many world problems.
Have you used generative-AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) for writing?
Your age:
Your gender:
Where do you currently live?
How would you rate your English reading ability?

pages.title: What this study was really about — please read
pages.reconsent_text: I have read this debrief and I am happy for my responses to be used in the research.
pages.html:
Thank you. Now that you have finished, we can explain the study fully.

This study examines whether a single disclosure label changes how readers respond to a piece of writing, even when the writing is identical. Some participants saw a tag reading “Created Using Generative AI” on the work page; others did not. This label was assigned at random.

In fact, all three stories used in this study were written by humans (members of our research team). No AI system wrote any of the stories, regardless of which tags you saw. We used a brief cover story (describing the study only as being about “reading and reactions”) because telling you the exact hypothesis in advance would change the very reactions we are trying to measure. We are sorry for this small, temporary deception.

Because the label you saw may have been inaccurate, we want to give you the choice to withdraw your data. You will be paid in full either way. If you would prefer that we do not use your responses, simply leave the box below unticked.

Questions or concerns? Contact the research team (details supplied on the Prolific study page). If reflecting on AI and creative work raised anything you'd like to discuss, you are welcome to get in touch.
